relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Again YHWH says: send my people to serve/worship.
Refusal is named as holding.
The hand of YHWH touches the economy of beasts.
A distinction is made between Israel and Egypt.
An appointed time is set.
The sign falls on Egypt's livestock, while Israel's are spared.
Pharaoh investigates, sees the distinction, and still hardens.
Soot from the kiln becomes the next sign.
The dust of oppression returns as painful exposure.
Boils break out on human and beast.
The magicians can no longer stand before Moses.
Pharaoh's heart is strengthened in refusal.
The morning demand returns: send my people.
The signs move toward Pharaoh's heart.
YHWH names restraint inside judgment.
Pharaoh is made to stand so YHWH's name may be known.
He still exalts himself over the people.
A heavy hail is announced.
A mercy-warning gives time to shelter life.
Some servants of Pharaoh revere the word and bring life inside.
Others do not set heart to the warning.
Moses stretches his hand toward heaven.
Thunder, hail, and fire descend.
The storm is unlike anything known in Egypt.
The field-world is struck.
Goshen remains apart from the hail.
Pharaoh confesses misalignment with his mouth.
He asks for intercession and promises release.
Moses' open palms will show that earth belongs to YHWH.
Yet Moses knows reverence has not truly entered.
The early crops are struck.
The later crops remain.
Moses spreads his palms, and the storm ceases.
Relief again becomes Pharaoh's return to hardness.
The heart remains hard, and the people are not sent.

contemplative_note: Exodus 9 deepens the discernment: some inside Egypt heed the warning and protect life, while Pharaoh confesses without turning.
